canonical_question: How do I implement Multi-Factor Authentication?

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: NIST SP 800-63-4 superseded 800-63-3 (2025-08); deprecated email OTP and downgraded SMS

constraints: TOTP shared secrets must be encrypted at rest (AES-256-GCM or equivalent) -- never store plaintext
Rate-limit verification attempts to 3-5 per minute per user to prevent brute-force
Backup/recovery codes are one-time-use -- hash them like passwords (bcrypt/argon2)
SMS OTP must not be the sole second factor -- NIST SP 800-63-4 deprecated SMS as standalone
WebAuthn relying party ID (rpID) must match the effective domain
TOTP time window tolerance should be +/- 1 step (30s) maximum

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Need passwordless-only login (no password at all)
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: WebAuthn/passkeys-only flow -- MFA assumes a primary factor already exists

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Need SSO federation (SAML/OIDC) with MFA delegated to IdP
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: OAuth2/OIDC with IdP-enforced MFA -- do not implement MFA in the relying party
Mutual TLS or API key + HMAC signing -- MFA is for interactive human authentication

alternative_to.label: OAuth2 Authorization Code with PKCE (delegates MFA to IdP)

often_confused_with.label: Passwordless Authentication (replaces passwords; MFA supplements them)

inputs_needed.question: Which MFA method do you want to implement?
What backend framework/language are you using?
What recovery mechanism do you need for lost authenticators?
options: Account recovery via verified email + identity verification
